children.heading: Cooperative agreements and grants for establishing and supporting
children.label: a Cooperative agreements and grants for establishing and supporting

children.text: The Director of the Institute may enter into cooperative agreements with and make grants to public or private nonprofit entities to pay all or part of the cost of planning, establishing, or strengthening, and providing basic operating support for centers for basic and clinical research into, training in, and demonstration of advanced diagnostic, prevention, control, and treatment methods for cancer.
A cooperative agreement or grant under paragraph (1) shall be entered into in accordance with policies established by the Director of NIH and after consultation with the Institute’s advisory council.

children.heading: Uses for Federal payments under cooperative agreements or grants
children.label: b Uses for Federal payments under cooperative agreements or grants

children.text: construction (notwithstanding any limitation under );section 289e of this title
staffing and other basic operating costs, including such patient care costs as are required for research;

clinical training, including training for allied health professionals, continuing education for health professionals and allied health professions personnel, and information programs for the public respecting cancer; and

children.heading: Period of support; additional periods
children.label: c Period of support; additional periods
children.text: Support of a center under subsection (a) may be for a period of not to exceed five years. Such period may be extended by the Director for additional periods of not more than five years each if the operations of such center have been reviewed by an appropriate technical and scientific peer review group established by the Director and if such group has recommended to the Director that such period should be extended.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: d Construction
children.text: Research centers under this section may not be considered centers of excellence for purposes of .section 282(b)(10) of this title
